text:
PARIS ГўВҖВ” Even Simone Biles sometimes gets caught up in the legend of Simone Biles.ГӮ

As she stood atop the podium during SaturdayГўВҖВҷs vault final, preparing to unleash the flips that would earn her her third gold medal of these Olympics and her 10th overall, her coach Laurent Landi signaled to her:Wait.

ГўВҖВңObviously once you get up there, your adrenaline is pumping,ГўВҖВқ she said afterward. ГўВҖВңHe wanted to make sure that I was staying calm and going on my own time, even though the thing said GO and everybody started cheering.ГўВҖВқ

As she has all Olympics, Biles blocked out the expectations of others and focused on her own. She took a breath and began sprinting down the runway. When she landed the Yurchenko double pike vault that bears her nameГўВҖВ”the Biles II, one of five eponymous skillsГўВҖВ”it was all but over.ГӮ

She sealed gold with a less complicated vault that made her combined score a preposterous 15.300. BrazilГўВҖВҷs Rebeca Andrade, the best gymnast on earth not named Biles, and the 2023 world champion in the vault, finished second with a score of 14.966. Jade Carey, who has spent the past three years reliving the instant when she stumbled on the runway in Tokyo and finished last in this event, took bronze.ГӮ

Biles hadlamented AndradeГўВҖВҷs skillsafter ThursdayГўВҖВҷs individual all-around final, saying, ГўВҖВңI donГўВҖВҷt want to compete with Rebeca no more! IГўВҖВҷm tired! SheГўВҖВҷs way too close.ГўВҖВқ On Saturday, after AndradeГўВҖВҷs first vault, Biles turned to Carey, grinned and shrugged dramatically, as if to say, ГўВҖВңWhat are we supposed to do about her?ГўВҖВқ

But Biles, 27, had already done what she needed to do, and everyone in Bercy Arena knew it. Her difficulty score is so high that as long as she stays on her feet, itГўВҖВҷs almost impossible for anyone else to match her. Andrade actually executed her vaults better, by more than two-tenths of a point, but it didnГўВҖВҷt matter.ГӮ

This was BilesГўВҖВҷs fourth Yurchenko double pike in competition this weekГўВҖВ”once in the qualifiers,once in the team final,once in the individual all-aroundГўВҖВ”and she said she would be retiring the skill.ГӮ

ГўВҖВңI mean, I kind of nailed that one,ГўВҖВқ she said with a grin.

As for herself, she was less decisive. ГўВҖВңNever say never,ГўВҖВқ she said. ГўВҖВңThe next Olympics is at home, so you never know. But I am getting really old.ГўВҖВқ

As she has all week, Biles set records in both age and decoration: She became the oldest woman to win gold in the vault since Ekaterina Kalinchuk, representing the Soviet Union, did so at 30 in 1952, the first time womenГўВҖВҷs gymnastics were contested at the Olympics. And BilesГўВҖВҷs gold today, her second on vault after she also won in Rio, tied her with runner Allyson Felix for second most golds by a U.S. woman. (Swimmers Jenny Thompson and Katie Ledecky each have eight.) Biles also now owns 40 Olympic and world championship medals, 30 of them gold.ГӮ

Although SaturdayГўВҖВҷs vaults were smooth, the process of getting there was not. In Tokyo, it was on the vault, of course, that the twisties that had dogged her for weeks became insurmountable. She fell out of her vaultГўВҖВ”an Amanar, which entails two and a half twistsГўВҖВ”during the team final and withdrew from event after event before stripping the twisting elements from her balance beam routine and gutting out a bronze medal. She took a year off from practicing and two off from competition before returning in 2023, and she initially performed the Amanar again, but she scrapped it in favor of the Biles II, which has two flips but no twists.ГӮ

That was at LandiГўВҖВҷs request, he said afterward, pointing out that even a one-point deduction for a fall on the Biles II would bring her score down only to the start value for the Amanar. "So what the hell?" he said.

ГўВҖВңI think weГўВҖВҷre both still a little bit traumatized [from] Tokyo,ГўВҖВқ Biles said. ГўВҖВңAnd weГўВҖВҷre excited that I can do multiple vaults, so that we donГўВҖВҷt have to use the two and a half.ГўВҖВқ

Otherwise, she said, her mental health is in good shape as she heads into her final two events here, MondayГўВҖВҷs beam final and floor exercise final. She has been speaking frequently with her therapist, despite the time difference, but she felt that she did not need the extra support on Saturday morning. She has insisted that after so many years of worrying about what other people thought, she is focused only on herself. She did, however, make time to comment on all the people who questioned her in Tokyo. ГўВҖВңTheyГўВҖВҷre really quiet right now,ГўВҖВқ she noted.ГӮ

Her fans are not. But gymnastics, Landi said, ГўВҖВңis in the head, more than the physical aspect.ГўВҖВқ And all week, Biles has been able to ignore the noise

Stephanie Apstein is a senior writer covering baseball and Olympic sports for Sports Illustrated, where she started as an intern in 2011 and has since covered a dozen World Series and three Olympics. She has twice won top honors from the Associated Press Sports Editors, and her work has been included in the Best American Sports Writing book series. She graduated from Trinity College with a bachelorГўВҖВҷs in French and Italian, and has a masterГўВҖВҷs in journalism from Columbia University.

title: Simone Biles Conquers the Vault to Capture Third Gold Medal of the Paris Olympics
description: The worldГўВҖВҷs most decorated gymnast nailed her routineГўВҖВ”which featured one of the five skills that bears her nameГўВҖВ”and earned her 10th overall Olympic medal.
